content_payload.instructions: You are a junior researcher on the Space Lab. A batch of 10 asteroid samples has arrived. Classify each asteroid based on its mineral composition, size, and orbit type. Work fast — the analysis window closes in 2 minutes!

speed.description: Time taken to complete all classifications

content_payload.scenario: You are the resource manager for a Mars colony of 200 settlers. A dust storm has damaged 40% of your solar panels and a supply ship is 45 days away. You must decide how to allocate remaining power across life support, food production, communications, and research.

decisions.question: How much power do you allocate to life support?
Do you ration food production or maintain current levels?
A researcher says she can repair panels if given 100 kWh for her lab. Do you approve?
Settlers are panicking. How do you communicate the situation?

risk_assessment.description: Balanced approach to survival vs. recovery

content_payload.brief: Design a self-sustaining space habitat for 50 people orbiting Jupiter's moon Europa. Your design must address: gravity simulation, energy source, food systems, psychological wellbeing, and emergency protocols.

deliverables.label: Habitat Layout
deliverables.description: Describe or sketch the physical layout of your habitat

deliverables.label: Energy Plan
deliverables.description: How will you generate and store energy?

deliverables.label: Food & Water
deliverables.description: Describe your food production and water recycling systems

deliverables.label: Crew Wellbeing
deliverables.description: How will you maintain morale and mental health?

deliverables.label: Emergency Protocol
deliverables.description: What happens if a critical system fails?

content_payload.instructions: A new Indian snack brand called 'Crunchify' needs a brand identity in 90 seconds! Pick the best combination of colors, tagline, and target audience.

brand_coherence.description: How well choices work together as a brand

content_payload.scenario: You are a film director at a Mumbai production house. You have been given a 3-minute short film slot at a prestigious festival. The theme is 'Unseen India'. Craft your story arc.

steps.label: Concept
steps.prompt: What is your film about? Describe the core idea in 2-3 sentences.

steps.label: Characters
steps.prompt: Describe your main character(s). What makes them compelling?

steps.label: Story Beats
steps.prompt: Outline 5 key story beats (opening, inciting incident, rising action, climax, resolution).

steps.label: Visual Style
steps.prompt: What visual style will you use? (e.g., handheld documentary, cinematic wide shots, animation)

steps.label: Impact
steps.prompt: What do you want the audience to feel after watching?

content_payload.brief: Your school wants to add a new feature to its student app. Design a feature that solves a real problem students face daily. Create wireframes (described in text), user flow, and a pitch.

deliverables.label: Problem Statement
deliverables.description: What specific student problem does this solve?

deliverables.label: Feature Description
deliverables.description: Describe the feature and how it works

deliverables.label: User Flow
deliverables.description: Step-by-step user journey through the feature

deliverables.label: Wireframe Description
deliverables.description: Describe 3 key screens of your feature

deliverables.label: Pitch
deliverables.description: 30-second pitch to the school principal

content_payload.instructions: Crack 5 pattern locks to advance through the dungeon. Each lock shows a number sequence — find the rule and predict the next number.

content_payload.scenario: The school's student database has been corrupted! As the IT detective, you must analyze the clues, find inconsistencies, and restore the correct data. You have access to 4 data tables with deliberate errors.

questions.question: List all data integrity issues you can find.
Which issues are most critical to fix first and why?
How would you prevent these issues in the future?

content_payload.brief: Design an algorithm (in pseudocode or plain English) to solve a real-world scheduling problem: your school has 30 teachers, 20 classrooms, and 8 periods per day. Create a timetable that minimizes conflicts.
constraints: No teacher can be in two rooms at once
No classroom can hold two classes at once
Each class must have all required subjects per week
Teachers have a maximum of 6 periods per day
At least 1 free period per teacher per day

deliverables.label: Approach
deliverables.description: Describe your algorithm strategy

deliverables.label: Pseudocode
deliverables.description: Write step-by-step pseudocode

deliverables.label: Edge Cases
deliverables.description: What edge cases did you consider?

deliverables.label: Optimization
deliverables.description: How would you optimize this further?

content_payload.instructions: You have 2 minutes to create a snap pitch for a startup idea. Pick a problem, name your startup, define your target customer, and state how you will make money.

prompts.label: Problem
prompts.prompt: What everyday problem in India are you solving?

prompts.label: Target Customer
prompts.prompt: Who is your ideal customer? Be specific.

prompts.label: One-liner Pitch
prompts.prompt: Pitch your startup in one sentence.

problem_validity.description: Is this a real, significant problem?

content_payload.scenario: You run a small chai brand called 'Chai-Wala Premium'. A large retail chain (MegaMart) wants to stock your chai but is driving a hard bargain. Navigate the negotiation to get the best deal while keeping the relationship intact.

rounds.situation: MegaMart offers Rs 80 per unit. Your cost is Rs 65 and you usually sell at Rs 120. They want exclusive rights in their stores.
rounds.question: What is your counter-offer?

rounds.situation: MegaMart says they can guarantee 10,000 units/month but want 90-day payment terms. Your cash flow needs 30-day terms.
rounds.question: How do you respond?

rounds.situation: MegaMart's competitor, QuickBasket, calls you offering Rs 95/unit but only 3,000 units/month with 30-day terms.
rounds.question: What do you do?

rounds.situation: MegaMart finds out about QuickBasket and threatens to end negotiations.
rounds.question: How do you handle this?

content_payload.brief: Create a one-page business plan for a student-run venture that can operate from your school campus. The venture must be legal, low-cost to start (under Rs 5,000), and solve a real need.

deliverables.label: Business Idea
deliverables.description: What is your venture and what problem does it solve?

deliverables.label: Customer Analysis
deliverables.description: Who will buy this? How many potential customers in your school?

deliverables.label: Cost Breakdown
deliverables.description: Itemize your startup costs (must stay under Rs 5,000)

deliverables.label: Revenue Projection
deliverables.description: How much will you charge? Project first month revenue.

deliverables.label: Growth Plan
deliverables.description: How will you scale this beyond your school?

deliverables.label: Risk Assessment
deliverables.description: What could go wrong and how will you handle it?

content_payload.instructions: Quick-fire choices! Pick the option that resonates with you more. There are no wrong answers.
Trust your gut! Pick the option that feels more 'you'. No right or wrong answers.

pairs.option_a: Take a big risk for big reward
